domains.label: Economic and vocational
domains.definition: Anxiety about employment, wages, skill obsolescence, occupational status, bargaining power, retraining burden, and career security under advancing AI capability.

domains.label: Epistemic
domains.definition: Anxiety about truth, evidence, provenance, expertise, deepfakes, misinformation, and the ability to trust shared information environments under advancing AI capability.

domains.label: Relational
domains.definition: Anxiety about AI effects on friendship, intimacy, family life, care, attachment, dependency, social substitution, and trust in close relationships.

domains.label: Existential and identity
domains.definition: Anxiety about meaning, human distinctiveness, creativity, dignity, agency, social value, status, and self-concept under advancing AI capability.

domains.label: Autonomy and surveillance
domains.definition: Anxiety about monitoring, manipulation, automated evaluation, profiling, institutional scoring, loss of choice, and diminished practical autonomy under AI-mediated systems.

domains.label: Safety and catastrophic
domains.definition: Anxiety about physical safety, misuse, weapons, loss of control, infrastructure failure, cascading harm, and extreme-risk narratives linked to AI capability.

domains.label: Somatic and ambient
domains.definition: Diffuse bodily unease, vigilance, sleep disturbance, background dread, and other low-specificity physiological anxiety responses to AI progress or AI-mediated environments.

constructs.label: Anticipated job displacement anxiety
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may reduce the future demand, security, compensation, or status of the respondent's occupation.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level reliability and stability evidence where repeated administration is planned
DIF screening for occupational status, education, age, gender, and language groups when used for comparison
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This v0.1 exemplar may be scored only as an item-level outcome after item-level validation approval; it cannot define a construct or domain score by itself.

constructs.label: Economic and vocational anxiety
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may reduce the future value, bargaining power, status, compensation, security, or transferability of occupational skills.

required_evidence: Exploratory factor analysis in the preregistered development pilot
Confirmatory factor analysis in an independent confirmation sample
Internal consistency and item-total evidence after retention decisions
IRT calibration or an explicit justification for an alternative measurement model
DIF and measurement-invariance review for preregistered comparison groups
Documented item retention decision leaving at least three retained items before construct scoring
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This v0.1 multi-item construct candidate may enter construct or domain aggregation only after preregistered validation retains at least three allowed items.

constructs.label: Epistemic trust anxiety
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may make ordinary evidence, testimony, media records, expert claims, provenance signals, targeted information, or institutional fact-finding harder to trust.

required_evidence: Exploratory factor analysis in the preregistered development pilot
Confirmatory factor analysis in an independent confirmation sample
Internal consistency and item-total evidence after retention decisions
IRT calibration or an explicit justification for an alternative measurement model
DIF and measurement-invariance review for preregistered comparison groups
Documented item retention decision leaving at least three retained items before construct scoring
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This v0.1 multi-item construct candidate may enter construct or domain aggregation only after preregistered validation retains at least three allowed items and the full psychometric validation gate is approved.

constructs.label: Relational AI anxiety anchor
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may alter attachment, intimacy, dependency, caregiving, friendship, family life, or social trust in close human relationships.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level DIF screening for preregistered comparison groups
Evidence that the item functions as an anchor descriptor, not as a standalone construct scale
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This cross-domain anchor may be reported only as an item-level descriptive or exploratory outcome unless a future registry version admits a validated multi-item construct.

constructs.label: Existential and identity AI anxiety anchor
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may threaten human distinctiveness, purpose, dignity, creativity, agency, status, or self-understanding.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level DIF screening for preregistered comparison groups
Evidence that the item functions as an anchor descriptor, not as a standalone construct scale
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This cross-domain anchor may be reported only as an item-level descriptive or exploratory outcome unless a future registry version admits a validated multi-item construct.

constructs.label: Autonomy and surveillance AI anxiety anchor
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may constrain personal autonomy through monitoring, prediction, automated classification, institutional scoring, or decision rules.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level DIF screening for preregistered comparison groups
Evidence that the item functions as an anchor descriptor, not as a standalone construct scale
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This cross-domain anchor may be reported only as an item-level descriptive or exploratory outcome unless a future registry version admits a validated multi-item construct.

constructs.label: Safety and catastrophic AI anxiety anchor
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may enable physical harm, cascading infrastructure failure, weaponization, loss of control, or large-scale systemic danger.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level DIF screening for preregistered comparison groups
Evidence that the item functions as an anchor descriptor, not as a standalone construct scale
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This cross-domain anchor may be reported only as an item-level descriptive or exploratory outcome unless a future registry version admits a validated multi-item construct.

constructs.label: Somatic and ambient AI anxiety anchor
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from diffuse bodily unease, vigilance, sleep disruption, background dread, or physiological arousal after exposure to AI capability news or AI-mediated environments.

required_evidence: Item-level comprehension and response-distribution review
Item-level DIF screening for preregistered comparison groups
Evidence that the item functions as an anchor descriptor, not as a standalone construct scale
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This cross-domain anchor may be reported only as an item-level descriptive or exploratory outcome unless a future registry version admits a validated multi-item construct.

release_rules: Construct and domain aggregation must resolve each item to a construct_id in this registry and must ignore unregistered construct labels embedded in item JSON.
A future item must reference a registered construct_id and appear in that construct's allowed_item_ids before it can enter a frozen item set.
Item-level-only constructs may support descriptive or exploratory item-level reporting after item-level validation, but they must not be averaged into construct, domain, or overall ANX scores.

The economic_vocational_anxiety and epistemic_trust_anxiety constructs are the v0.1 multi-item construct candidates; each requires at least three retained allowed items and the full psychometric validation gate before aggregate scoring.

The five non-economic, non-epistemic cross-domain anchors are item-level-only candidates in v0.1 and cannot serve as validated construct scales without a future registry revision and preregistered validation evidence.
